Yolulo Operations
Merchant Setup

New Merchant Onboarding

Step-by-step configuration guide for adding a new merchant to the Hyperzod platform using the Yolulo model

Platform Hyperzod Admin
Model Yolulo
Version 1.0
Updated March 2026
Overview 01 Navigate 02 Details 03 Commission 04 Custom & SEO 05 Zone 06 Timing 07 General 08 Orders 09 Users 10 Payouts ✓ Checklist
Getting Started

Setup Overview

Complete all 10 steps in order. Some settings in later steps depend on earlier configuration being in place.

STEP 01
🧭
Navigate to Merchants
Access the merchant management hub from the main sidebar
STEP 02
📋
Merchant Details
Name, address, contact info, order types and delivery settings
STEP 03
💰
Commission & Taxes
Set commission rates per order type and configure tax settings
STEP 04
✏️
Custom Text & SEO
Custom message, SEO slug, checkout fields and timezone
STEP 05
📍
Serviceable Zone
Define the delivery coverage area on the map
STEP 06
🕐
Store Timing
Set business hours and active trading days
STEP 07
⚙️
General Settings
Delivery rates, pickup time, notifications and order config
STEP 08
📦
Order Settings
Order types, scheduling, tipping and delivery instructions
STEP 09
👥
Users & Permissions
Invite team members and assign role-based access
STEP 10
💳
Payout Method
Connect Stripe for automated merchant payouts
Step 01

Navigate to Merchants

Open the Hyperzod admin panel and click Merchants in the left navigation sidebar.

Fig 1.1 — Main navigation sidebar with Merchants highlighted

Fig 1.2 — Merchants page header: Merchant / Category / AI Recommendations tabs

Fig 1.3 — Merchants list. Click "+ Create New" to begin

Step 02

Create New Merchant — Details

Click "+ Create New" and complete all required fields in the Add Merchant form.

Basic Details
Merchant Type Required
Ecommerce (or appropriate type)
Language
English
Name Required
Enter the merchant's full trading name
Address / Postcode
Full location details including Country, State and City
Contact Number Required
Primary merchant contact number and email

Fig 2.1 — Add Merchant: type, name, address and contact fields

Order & Delivery Settings
Category
Select the correct merchant category
Order Types Required
Select all applicable — Delivery, Pickup, Custom 2, etc.
Default Order Type
Set the primary order type shown to customers
Status Required
Inactive — leave until all configuration is complete
Delivery By
Admin
Delivery Provider
Autozod
Zones
Assign the relevant zone for this merchant

Fig 2.2 — Order types, delivery provider (Autozod), zone and Status fields

⚠️
Important: Keep Status set to Inactive until every step in this document has been completed. Only set to Active once the final checklist has been verified.
Step 03

Commission & Taxes

Scroll down on the Add Merchant form to configure commission rates and tax settings.

Commission — set per order type tab
Order Type Tabs
Select each tab in turn: Delivery / Pickup / Custom / Custom 2
Commission Rate
Enter Percentage (%) and/or Fixed (£) amount per order type
Taxes
Include tax in prices
Check if product and delivery prices already include VAT

Fig 3.1 — Commission by order type tab and Tax configuration

Step 04

Custom Text, SEO & Timezone

Complete the remaining fields then click Save to create the merchant record.

Remaining Fields
Custom Message
Up to 40 characters — optional storefront-facing message
SEO Slug
Auto-populates from merchant name — edit if needed
Checkout Custom Fields
Create merchant-specific checkout fields, or leave empty
Timezone
Europe/London — adjust to merchant's local timezone

Fig 4.1 — Custom Text, SEO slug, Checkout Fields and Timezone

💡
After saving: the merchant appears in the Merchants list. All further configuration is done via the merchant's own settings panel — accessed through the edit icon or Login button in the list.
Step 05

Merchant Serviceable Zone

Define the geographical area this merchant can deliver to. Customers outside this zone cannot place orders.

Zone Configuration
Merchant Address
Search and pin the merchant's location on the map
Area Type
Radius for circular coverage · Geofence for irregular shapes
Radius
4 Mi — adjust to match operational coverage

Fig 5.1 — Serviceable Zone: 4 mile radius with merchant pinned in Preston

Step 06

Store Timing

Set the merchant's operating hours. These control when the store shows as open on the customer storefront.

Business Hours
Enable Business Hours
Enabled
Active Days
Set open and close times using the time pickers for each trading day
Split Shifts
Use "+ Add Shift" to add a second shift for lunch breaks or split trading
Closed Days
Toggle off for any days the merchant does not trade

Fig 6.1 — Store Timing: Mon–Fri active, Saturday and Sunday toggled off

Step 07

General Settings

Navigate to Settings → General in the merchant admin panel.

7a — Delivery Configurations
Pickup Time Text
Enabled — set to 15 minutes
Order Delivery Rates
Select Per Unit — distance-based pricing

Fig 7.1 — Delivery Configurations: Per unit selected, pickup time 15 min

7b — Delivery Rate Slabs
Unit of Measurement
Mi (miles)
Base Fee
£3.00 — charged on all orders regardless of distance
Rate — 0 to 1 mi
£0.00/mi
Rate — 1 mi onwards
£1.20/mi

Fig 7.2 — Distance slabs: base £3, then £1.20/mi from 1 mile

7c — Notifications
Notification Emails
Enter order alert email addresses, comma-separated
Mobile Number
Add for SMS notifications if required

Fig 7.3 — Notification: email recipients configured for order alerts

7d — Order Configurations
Minimum Order Value
£0 — no minimum (adjust as required)
Auto Reject Order
Enabled — 5 minutes
Auto Accept Order
Disabled — merchant accepts orders manually
Default Preparation Time
Enabled — 30 minutes

Fig 7.4 — Order Configurations: auto-reject 5 min, prep time 30 min

Step 08

Order Settings

Navigate to Settings → Order to configure order types, scheduling, tipping and charges.

8a — Accept Orders & Scheduling
Accept Orders
Enabled
Custom Message
"Accept Orders" or a custom customer-facing message
Order Scheduling
Enabled — customers can schedule orders in advance

Fig 8.1 — Accept Orders enabled, Delivery Instructions enabled with options

8b — Order Tip
Order Tip
Enabled
Tip Required
Unchecked — tipping is optional
Tip Type
Tip by fixed amount
Tip Values
£5 · £10 · £15

Fig 8.2 — Order Tip: fixed amounts £5 / £10 / £15 configured

8c — Order Types
Order TypeModeStatus
DeliveryExpressEnabled
PickupExpress PickupEnabled
Pick & DropStandardEnabled
CustomStandardDisabled
Custom 2Delivery RequiredEnabled

Fig 8.3 — Order Types: Delivery, Pickup, Pick & Drop, Custom 2 enabled

8d — Checkout Fields & Custom Charges
Checkout Custom Fields
Leave empty unless merchant-specific fields are required
Transaction Fee
Enabled — applicable to Merchant

Fig 8.4 — Custom Fields empty, Transaction Fee charge enabled

Step 09

Users & Permissions

Invite team members and assign role-based access levels appropriate to each person's responsibilities.

Fig 9.1 — Users & Permissions list: users showing Pending invitation status

Fig 9.2 — Add User modal: email entry with permission modules

Available Permission Modules
Dashboard / Orders
Core operational access
Merchants
Merchants · Category · Product
Customers / Promotions
Customer data · Promotions · Coupons · Popups · Campaigns
Settings (22)
General · Order · Payment · Store Timing · Notifications · Zones · Payouts · and more
Apps / Plans / Autozod
Platform management modules
Reports / Logs
Reports · Export Reports · Payment Logs

Fig 9.3 — Permissions: Dashboard, Orders, Merchants, Customers

Fig 9.4 — Permissions: Settings module with all 22 sub-permissions

Fig 9.5 — Permissions: Plans, Autozod, Help, General Permissions

Fig 9.6 — Permissions: Promotions, Reviews, Logs and Reports modules

⚠️
Pending status: Users show as Pending until they accept the invitation. Use "Resend Invite" if no response after 24 hours.
Step 10

Payout Method — Stripe Connect

Connect the merchant's Stripe account to enable automated payouts. Required before going live.

Stripe Connect Setup
Navigate to
Settings → Payouts
Select Method
Stripe Connect
Onboarding
Follow the Stripe Connect flow to link the merchant's account
Confirmation
Connected — green status badge confirms successful link

Fig 10.1 — Payouts Method: Stripe Connect showing Connected status

Final Step

Activation Checklist

Confirm every item before setting the merchant Status to Active.

DETAILS
Merchant name, address, contact info, order types, delivery provider (Autozod) and zone all saved
COMMISSION
Commission rates configured for all applicable order types; tax inclusion confirmed
ZONE
Serviceable zone defined on the map with correct radius or geofence boundary
TIMING
Business hours set correctly per day; closed days toggled off
GENERAL
Delivery rate slabs, pickup time (15 min), notification emails, auto-reject (5 min), prep time (30 min) all configured
ORDERS
Order types correct, Scheduling on, Tip configured, Transaction Fee enabled
USERS
All team members invited with correct permissions assigned; invitations accepted or resent
STRIPE
Stripe Connect payout method connected and showing green Connected status
ACTIVATE
Merchant Status changed from Inactive to Active
TEST
End-to-end test order placed and completed successfully before going live
Yolulo

Hyperzod Platform · New Merchant Setup SOP · v1.0 · March 2026

Internal Use Only
